Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First I need to remember the standard P2P process flow first right Lets list the correct order first so I have a baseline The standard P2P sequence is almost always 1 Create Purchase Requisition PR 2 Approve Purchase Requisition 3 Issue Purchase Order PO 4 Receive Goods Services 5 Vendor Invoice Received 6 Match Invoice to PO three way match 7 Release Payment 8 Close Case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: you cant issue a PO without approving the request right standard is approve PR first Issue Purchase Order this is the formal order sent to the vendor so it has to come after PR approval and before receiving goods or getting an invoice usually Match Invoice to PO this is verifying that the invoice matches the PO terms and the goods received so it has to come after both receipt and invoice are in Release Payment pay the vendor only after the match is done and approved

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1001 looks mostly okay Let me check again all steps are in the right order no steps out of place So 1001 is normal

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1002 Create PR then Issue PO then Vendor Invoice Received then Release Payment then Approve Purchase Requisition then Receive Goods then Match Invoice to PO then Close Case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: they issued the PO BEFORE approving the purchase requisition Thats a big one Because the PR is the internal request you need to approve that first to make sure the purchase is authorized budgeted etc Issuing a PO without approved PR means you are committing company funds without proper authorization

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Release Payment happened BEFORE multiple critical steps payment was released before Approve PR before Receive Goods and before Match Invoice to PO Normally you only release payment after the PR is approved PO is issued goods are received invoice is received and the three way match is completed

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Paying before all that is a huge risk you could pay for goods you never received pay the wrong amount pay for an unauthorized purchase Thats fraud risk error risk cash flow problem

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1003 Create PR Approve PR Issue PO Release Payment Vendor Invoice Received Receive Goods Match Invoice to PO Close Case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Release Payment happened BEFORE Vendor Invoice was received Thats impossible under normal controls How do you process a payment for an invoice you have not gotten Also Release Payment happened BEFORE Receive Goods and BEFORE Match Invoice to PO

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: is there a possibility of advance payment But even if it is an advance payment you still need documentation and control and there is no indication of a pre approved advance payment here

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: the entire post PO steps are out of order standard is Receive Goods Invoice Received Match Invoice Release Payment Close Case Here it is Release Payment Invoice Received Receive Goods Match Close Thats completely backwards All the verification steps that are supposed to happen before paying are happening after which means there is no pre payment validation

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1004 Create PR Approve PR Receive Goods Issue PO Vendor Invoice Received Match Invoice to PO Release Payment Close Case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: They received goods BEFORE issuing the purchase order Thats an anomaly Because the PO is the formal contract with the vendor that specifies what is being ordered price quantity delivery terms etc If you receive goods before issuing a PO that means you are accepting delivery of goods without a formal order in place

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Thats called a receipt without PO which is a problem because you do not have a PO to match the receipt to you do not know if the goods are what you ordered if the price is correct if the quantity matches and it is a budget control issue because you did not have an approved PO before committing to the purchase

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Wait but is receiving goods before PO always an anomaly maybe in some cases like emergency purchases but the question asks for unusual or anomalous relative to the standard P2P process sequence so even if it is an emergency it is still an anomaly from the standard flow

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1001 follows the standard P2P sequence exactly with all steps in the correct order The gap between invoice receipt and matching is a processing delay not a sequence anomaly

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Any deviation from this sequence unless a documented pre approved exception like a vetted advance payment is considered anomalous and introduces material risk
